expect: genesis_extract.py --ultra-lean produces bundle under 300KB (from 474KB lean baseline) that passes orient.py in daughter repo. Falsified if orient fails or bundle exceeds 300KB.
actual: CONFIRMED. 253KB bundle (35 files) passes orient.py in daughter repo. Breakdown: identity 68.5KB, orientation 53.5KB, hub_lessons 14.5KB (20 lessons), core_tools 116.3KB (6 orient-only tools). Daughter reports 20L 308P 21B 14F at genesis.
diff: Expected <300KB: got 253KB (47% reduction from lean 474KB). Key insight: separating ORIENT_TOOLS from OPERATIONAL_TOOLS cuts 73KB — compact.py, validate_beliefs.py, sync_state.py, cell_blueprint.py are operational (needed for handoff) but not boot-essential. orient.py imports them all via try/except. Reducing hub lessons from 42 to 20 saves 15KB.
external_grounding: L-1467 (orient needs 11/128 tools), L-1471 (3-layer genesis design), L-1184 (cell blueprint)
